site.title: Sangwoo Eom
site.tagline: Arctic mercury biogeochemist with expertise in photoredox kinetics and air-sea Hg(0) exchange
site.subtagline: Ph.D. candidate in Environmental and Energy Engineering at GIST, building a research portfolio around marine mercury redox cycling from estuaries to the Arctic Ocean.

contact.location: Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology (GIST), Republic of Korea
contact.address: 123 Cheomdangwagi-ro, Buk-gu, Gwangju 61005, Republic of Korea

bio: I study how light, inorganic carbon, dissolved organic matter, and particles regulate mercury redox cycling in marine systems.

My work combines lab photochemistry, trace-metal clean sampling, continuous dissolved gaseous mercury measurements, and multi-compartment modeling to connect process-level kinetics with large-scale ocean behavior.
Across contaminated estuaries and ice-influenced Arctic waters, I focus on the ocean-atmosphere interface where mercury is transformed, exchanged, and redistributed.

focusAreas.title: Redox Chemistry of Trace Elements in the Ocean
focusAreas.description: Elucidating mercury redox mechanisms mediated by superoxide, carbonate radicals, and halogen radicals under dark and light conditions.

focusAreas.title: Particle Effects on Marine Biogeochemistry
focusAreas.description: Investigating how particle-adsorbed metals, organic matter, and genomic composition influence marine biogeochemical processes.

focusAreas.title: Air-Sea Flux Chamber Development
focusAreas.description: Designing flux chambers that capture wave and microbubble effects and benchmarking in-situ measurements against Fick's law estimates.

projects.title: Photoreduction mechanisms of dissolved divalent mercury in surface seawater
projects.outcome: Demonstrated enhancing roles of carbonate and thiols, and the suppressing role of aromatic DOM on Hg(II) photoreduction in seawater.

projects.title: Air-sea exchange, sediment methylation, and ecosystem sources of mercury in the Arctic Ocean
projects.support: Korean Institute of Marine Science & Technology (KIMST)
projects.outcome: Estimated air-sea Hg(0) exchange across sea-ice regimes and linked photoredox kinetics to DOM composition in Arctic seawater.

projects.title: Investigation of water qualities and sediment contamination in Hyeongsan River Estuary
projects.outcome: Characterized biogeochemical cycling of dissolved and particulate mercury in a contaminated estuary.

projects.title: Rainwater mercury monitoring through the Asia-Pacific Mercury Monitoring Network
projects.outcome: Tracked potential mercury source regions in wet deposition across South Korea.

skills.description: Photoredox rate-constant estimation, continuous dissolved gaseous mercury measurements, and seawater photo-experimental setup design.
Trace-element clean water and sediment sampling, ocean-atmosphere interface measurements, and Arctic Araon cruise operations.
CVAFS workflows using BrooksRand and Tekran 2537 series for THg, MeHg, Hg(0), and methylation-demethylation kinetics.
DOC by VarioTOC, SPE for FT-ICR-MS, and FDOM analysis with Hitachi F-2500.
Chlorophyll-a, suspended particulate matter, UV-Vis, acid volatile sulfide, YSI meter, glove box, and ferrioxalate actinometry.
R, MATLAB, Excel, QGIS, Ocean Data View, SigmaPlot, SPSS, MINEQL+5.0, Mercury Guru, Hg Logger Plus, and MATLAB-based Hg modeling.
